spqr_replog.text: SLANDER FAILED: A campaign against Titus Pullo failed to take hold.
SLANDER FAILED: A campaign against Titus Pullo failed to take hold.
PARTIAL SLANDER: Rumours circulate about Titus Pullo, but the impact is limited. Reputation -2.

Titus Pullo spent 50T on Small Counter-Campaign against scandal "Weak rumour: Titus Pullo speaks loudly of Rome, yet whispers grow that his loyalty is to his own purse before the Republic. While honest senators sacrifice wealth and honour for the war against Hannibal, Pullo is said to hide behind empty patriotic words, seeking office, influence and profit while others bleed for Italy. Some now ask whether such a man serves Rome or only himself.".
PARTIAL SLANDER: Rumours circulate about Titus Pullo, but the impact is limited. Reputation -8.
SLANDER SPREADS: Lucius Vorenus  has been publicly marked by rumour: "He is not my friend". Reputation -10.
Titus Pullo spent 40T/0M on Temple Offering and gained +3 reputation.
Titus Pullo spent 70T/40M on Grain Charity and gained +6 reputation.
Paulus Maximus spent 70T/40M on Grain Charity and gained +6 reputation.
SLANDER SPREADS: Fabius Minimus has been publicly marked by rumour: "Fabius Minimus wants the monarchy back and wants the executive power all for himself". Reputation -5.
Horatio Marcellus Agrippa spent 40T/0M on Temple Offering and gained +3 reputation.
Abner Wasso Solidus spent 40T/0M on Temple Offering and gained +3 reputation.
Tiberius Anthony Corvinus spent 90T/0M on Veteran Patronage and gained +8 reputation.
Quintus Ulpius Silanus spent 70T/40M on Grain Charity and gained +6 reputation.
Lucius Vipsanius spent 40T/0M on Temple Offering and gained +3 reputation.
Quintus Fabius Numerius spent 70T/40M on Grain Charity and gained +6 reputation.

boostActions.desc: Public piety and visible offerings to the gods.
boostActions.name: Temple Offering

boostActions.desc: Distribute grain or aid to poorer citizens and clients.
boostActions.name: Grain Charity

boostActions.desc: Support veterans, wounded soldiers and military families.
boostActions.name: Veteran Patronage

boostActions.desc: Sponsor public spectacle, games and public goodwill.
boostActions.name: Public Games

counterActions.desc: Speeches, denials and friendly clients defending your name.
counterActions.name: Small Counter-Campaign

counterActions.desc: Public defense, witnesses, charity and pressure on the rumour network.
counterActions.name: Major Rehabilitation

slanderActions.desc: Forum gossip, tavern talk and whispers among clients.
slanderActions.name: Minor Rumour

slanderActions.desc: Paid informants, pamphlets, witnesses and coordinated rumours.
slanderActions.name: Organized Slander

slanderActions.desc: A large political attack that can ruin a candidate if believed.
slanderActions.name: Major Scandal Campaign

spqr_wealthlog.text: Aulus Domitius Ahenobarbus bought 95M food from the Roman state market for 190T gold.
Gaius Sempronius Gracchus bought 20M food from the Roman state market for 40T gold.
Titus Flaminius Vercus bought 6M food from the Roman state market for 12T gold.
Titus Pullo proposed treasury action: Set estate taxes to 10% gold and 5% food. Awaiting approval by the other Quaestor.
Lucius Vorenus  rejected treasury action: Set estate taxes to 10% gold and 5% food.
Lucius Vorenus  proposed treasury action: Set estate taxes to 10% gold and 5% food. Awaiting approval by the other Quaestor.
Titus Pullo approved treasury action: Set estate taxes to 10% gold and 5% food.
Lucretius Valerius Laurentius transferred 120T gold to Aulus Domitius Ahenobarbus.
Titus Pullo updated the Roman food market: 450M available at 2T per 1M.
Titus Pullo opened a 48h auction for 🧺 Market Stall in Latium. Seller: Titus Pullo. Starting bid: 500T gold.
Lucius Vorenus  bid 502T gold for 🧺 Market Stall in Latium.
Titus Pullo set the Roman food market to 10% of the state stockpile: 432M currently available at 2T per 1M.
Caius Herminus Aenaes sold 50M food to the Roman state for 25T gold. State: +50M food, -25T gold.

spqr_treasury_actions.note: Due to the current threat facing our great city, more gold for more steel will be raised. Decadence in peace is shamful. Decadence in war is deadly.
